original_text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, encouraging foreign investment.
identified_property: magnitude and quality of foreign investment attracted

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, drawing only negligible foreign interest limited to speculative, short-term capital flows with little productive value.

variations.label: low
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, attracting a small amount of foreign investment mostly concentrated in low-value, labor-intensive sectors with limited economic spillovers.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, attracting moderate foreign investment from mid-tier firms, though largely in conventional industries without significant technology transfer.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, encouraging foreign investment.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, attracting substantial foreign investment from well-established multinational firms bringing meaningful capital commitments and skilled job creation.

variations.label: high
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, attracting large-scale foreign investment from leading global corporations that bring advanced technology transfer, high-skilled employment, and long-term strategic commitments.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: A new tax reform reduces corporate tax rates to 15%, attracting transformative foreign investment from world-class multinationals establishing flagship R&D centers, catalyzing an innovation ecosystem with extensive technology transfer, premium job creation, and deep long-term economic integration.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier describes the same tax reform scenario but varies the quality and magnitude of foreign investment from negligible speculative capital (tier 1) through conventional investment (tiers 2-3), the neutral original (tier 4), to increasingly high-quality, strategic, and transformative investment (tiers 5-7). Distinctions are made along dimensions of investor prestige, technology transfer, job quality, commitment duration, and broader economic impact.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because it represents the highest quality and magnitude of foreign investment: world-class firms establishing deep, long-term commitments with R&D centers, technology transfer, premium employment, and systemic economic benefits—maximizing the return on the tax policy change.
